rationale: This is a puzzle/sort "relaxation" hook (Bus Jam-style hand-with-stylus gameplay) that fundamentally misrepresents Helix Jump's reflex-based rotating/bouncing core loop — running it would drive misleading installs and poor D1 retention. Visual frictions: muted blue/orange palette and top-down grid composition clash with Helix's neon vertical tower aesthetic, and the stylus-hand prop implies a slow tactile puzzle rather than a twitch arcade game. Only the broad casual audience overlaps, but the "feel relaxed" promise is the opposite of Helix's energetic/competitive mood — do not adapt this archetype.
